steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes highlight a specific numerical difference: the fennec fox has 64 chromosomes while the red fox has 34. The answer must use both numbers to compare the two species.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Genes, which are in fennec fox chromosomes, carry critical instructions for determining the fox's physical traits.: omits the chromosome numbers entirely. ✗
  • Choice B — While both are species of foxes, the fennec fox and the red fox don't have the same number of chromosomes: fennec foxes have sixty-four, whereas red foxes have only thirty-four.: names both fox species and contrasts 64 versus 34 chromosomes directly. ✓
  • Choice C — The fennec fox and the red fox both have chromosomes, which contain genes.: describes only one fox or fails to make the comparison. ✗
  • Choice D — The red fox, a species of fox, has thirty-four cellular structures called chromosomes.: buries or omits the key numerical contrast. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
